BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Bay Area Community Services Housing Corp

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: Improve the quality of life for the people in Alameda and Conta Costa Counties, California with specific needs imposed by age or disability.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Improve the quality of life for individuals in Alameda County, Contra Costa County, Solano County, Monterey County, Sacramento County and the surrounding area in the State of California.
IRS990.Desc: Housing and housing related services, including the rehabilitation and construction of low to moderate income and affordable housing, for behavioral health individuals including teens, adults, older adults, and families.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization is exempt from Federal and State Income taxes under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(3) and California Franchise Tax Board Code Section 23701d. Therefore, no provision for income taxes has been made in the accompanying consolidated financial statements. In addition, the Internal Revenue Service has determined The Organization is not a "private foundation" within the meaning of Section 509(a) of the Internal Revenue Code. Management of the Organization considers the likelihood of changes by taxing authorities in its filed tax returns and recognizes a liability for or discloses potential significant changes if management believes it is more likely than not for a change to occur, including changes to the Organization's status as a not-forprofit entity. Management believes the Organization met the requirements to maintain its tax-exempt status and has no income subject to unrelated business income tax. The Organization's tax returns for the past three years are subject to examination by tax authorities and may change upon examination.
Revenue from Other Affiliates of Organization 1,879,482.
Expenses from Other Affiliates of Organization 1,773,229.

Bay Area Community Services strives to maintain both internal and external equity. Internal equity is the same pay range. External equity is maintained by comparing wages to what other similarly situated organizations (non-profits) pay for comparable positions. It is the goal of Bay Area Community Services to eventually arrive at wages that are at the 75th percentile of comparable positions in the non-profit sector.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, line 7a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Board of Directors is appointed by the board of directors of the Bay Area Community Services, inc, a 501(c)(3) organization.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Form 990 is reviewed by the CFO and Executive Director. The Executive Director briefs the board of directors and gets the board's concurrence prior to submission.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Policies and procedures are disseminated to all of the working sites of bay area community services. These include personnel policies, accounting policies, petty cash procedures in addition to program procedures required for each site dependent on contract requirements.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization strives to maintain both internal and external equity. Internal equity is the same pay range. External equity is maintained by comparing wages to what other similarly situated organizations (nonprofits) pay for comparable positions. It is the goal of the organization to eventually arrive at wages that are at 75th percentile of comparable positions in the non profit sector.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The governing documents, conflict of interest policy and financial statements are made available upon request.
The organization has a committee that assumes responsibility for oversight of the audit.
